source_pr.title: executor: fix a order by bug

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select a from t use index(b) order by b").Check(testkit.Rows("9", "8", "7", "6", "5", "4", "3", "2", "1", "0")) (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select a from t use index(b) order by b").Check(testkit.Rows("9", "8", "7", "6", "5", "4", "3", "2", "1", "0")) (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select a from t use index(b) order by b").Check(testkit.Rows("9", "8", "7", "6", "5", "4", "3", "2", "1", "0")) (executor/executor_test.go)
